details.body: In `stage_consumer_guidance()` (task_prompt_contract.rs:63), the period is inside the backtick code span: `` `bead_execution_prompt/1.` ``. Move it outside: change the format string from `"{BEAD_TASK_PROMPT_CONTRACT_NAME}/{}.`"` to `"{BEAD_TASK_PROMPT_CONTRACT_NAME}/{}`."` so the rendered text is `` `bead_execution_prompt/1`. `` — otherwise LLM consumers or any future parser that extracts the contract identifier from this guidance text would include the trailing period as part of the name.

The `already_planned_elsewhere` field on `BeadProjectContext` is always hardcoded to `Vec::new()` in the only production construction site (cli/project.rs:340). The bead data model has no corresponding field to populate it. This means the `Already Planned Elsewhere` contract section will always render its default fallback text in production. Consider either (a) wiring it to bead dependency data (e.g. non-blocking dependencies), (b) parsing related beads from the milestone bundle, or (c) documenting it as a placeholder for future data wiring so the gap is intentional and tracked.

Extend the task-prompt contract rollout beyond `build_stage_prompt()`. Right now only the normal stage-execution path injects the shared contract guidance (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:109-113,163-174`), while prompt review still rewrites `prompt.md` through generic templates (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:89-125`, `src/contexts/workspace_governance/template_catalog.rs:121-145`) and final-review/completion still hand raw project prompts to reviewers/completers (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:227-236,1577-1592`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/completion.rs:102-129,248-256`, `src/contexts/workspace_governance/template_catalog.rs:181-205,215-229`). Because downstream detection is an exact marker match (`src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:53-55`), any prompt-review rewrite that drops or reformats that line silently disables the contract path. As shipped, the workflow still does not share one canonical bead contract end-to-end, and later decision stages can judge scope without the same authoritative `Must-Do Scope` / `Explicit Non-Goals` / `Already Planned Elsewhere` / `Review Policy` framing that implementer stages now receive.

Preserve milestone `agents_guidance` verbatim instead of wrapping it in a synthesized bullet list. The new renderer prefixes the entire guidance blob with `- ` (`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:560-568`), but `MilestoneBundle.agents_guidance` is free-form markdown and is rendered raw elsewhere (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:60-62,949-952`). Multi-line guidance, headings, fenced code blocks, or nested lists will now be mangled into a malformed list item inside `AGENTS / Repo Guidance`, which can hide or reinterpret repo-specific instructions in the canonical prompt.

In `validate_canonical_prompt_shape` (task_prompt_contract.rs:131), the variable `headings` is misleading — it contains ALL trimmed lines, not just headings. Rename to `trimmed_lines` or `lines` for clarity, since this function is part of the public validation contract and will be read by future maintainers.

High: `render_bead_task_prompt` inserts `bead_description` verbatim into `## Must-Do Scope` (`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:540-543,588-592`), but `validate_canonical_prompt_shape` locates each contract heading by the first matching line anywhere in the prompt (`src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:122-145`). Real bead descriptions already contain the repo-required `## Acceptance Criteria` marker, e.g. `.beads/issues.jsonl:65`, so a canonical prompt for those beads contains an embedded `## Acceptance Criteria` before the real contract section. After validators accept, `execute_prompt_review` re-validates the refined prompt (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:241-254`) and will reject it as "out of order" even though the prompt is otherwise valid. The generator needs to normalize/strip embedded contract headings before rendering, or the validator needs to scan only the top-level contract sections.

Medium: The shared consumer guidance says `Must-Do Scope` + `Acceptance Criteria` define in-scope work and `Explicit Non-Goals` is the out-of-scope section (`src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:66-72`), but the generator still fills `Explicit Non-Goals` only from `milestone_non_goals` and dumps the full bead description into `Must-Do Scope` (`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:540-547`). Real bead descriptions already carry bead-local `Non-goals:` blocks, e.g. `.beads/issues.jsonl:66`, so those deferred items stay inside `Must-Do Scope` and are presented to downstream stages as in-scope work. The contract generator should extract bead-local non-goals from the description or otherwise keep them out of the in-scope section.

In `split_bead_description_scope` (service.rs:577-580), the inner `section_kind_for_bead_description_line(line)` call is unreachable dead code. Line 570 already checked the same function and got `None` (otherwise the outer `continue` on line 572 would have fired). The inner block can never execute. Remove lines 577-580 or restructure to avoid the redundant call, as it misleads readers into thinking there is a transition path through it.

`validate_canonical_prompt_shape()` no longer guarantees that the canonical headings are unique and truly in order. In `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:131-144`, it now scans forward from the previous match and accepts the first later occurrence of each heading, so a malformed prompt with an earlier stray `## Acceptance Criteria` (or any other canonical heading) still passes as long as the heading appears again later in the expected slot. The new test at `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:218-225` explicitly codifies that permissive behavior. Because `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:245-253` uses this function as the final gate before accepting a refined prompt, prompt review can now persist contract-drifted prompts that still violate the promised canonical section order.

The diff deletes the checked-in repo workspace config `.ralph-burning/workspace.toml` (whole file removed), but repo-root commands still unconditionally require that file via `src/contexts/workspace_governance/mod.rs:65-66`. In a clean export of `HEAD`, running `ralph-burning config show` from the repo root now fails with `No such file or directory`, while the same command succeeds from `origin/master`. Unless this repo is intentionally no longer supposed to be an initialized workspace, removing the tracked workspace scaffold is a regression unrelated to the prompt-contract feature.

`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:500-509` and `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:564-583` make the bead-description splitter treat any colon-terminated label such as `Examples:` or `Notes:` as a section boundary. Inside `Non-goals:` or `## Acceptance Criteria`, that silently exits the section and pushes the following bullets back into `Must-Do Scope`. A bead description shaped like `Non-goals:\nExamples:\n- do not change CLI` currently renders `- do not change CLI` as in-scope work, which inverts the contract boundary.

`src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:131-145` validates canonical headings by scanning raw trimmed lines, while `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:699-704` injects milestone `agents_guidance` verbatim and `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:377-381` writes the rendered prompt without validating it. Any AGENTS block or fenced example that contains a canonical heading like `## Review Policy` is treated as a duplicate contract section, so a legitimate repo-guidance snippet can generate a prompt that later fails prompt review in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:245-253` even though the guidance itself is valid.

This branch deletes the tracked workspace scaffold `.ralph-burning/workspace.toml` (and `.ralph-burning/active-project`), but repo-root commands still unconditionally read `.ralph-burning/workspace.toml` in `src/contexts/workspace_governance/mod.rs:65-66`. In a clean `git archive HEAD`, `cargo run --quiet -- config show` now fails with `No such file or directory`, so the branch no longer works from a clean checkout even though `origin/master` does.

In `validate_canonical_prompt_shape` (task_prompt_contract.rs:134-185), when a section is missing, `expected_index` never advances past the gap. This causes every subsequent section — even if present and in order — to produce a spurious "unexpected canonical heading" error AND be reported as "missing" (because `seen_positions` is only set in the matching branch). A single missing section cascades into (N-remaining × 2) misleading errors. Fix: when `found_index > expected_index`, record any skipped indices as missing, advance `expected_index` to `found_index + 1`, and set `seen_positions[found_index]`. This keeps validation correct (still rejects the prompt) while producing accurate, actionable error messages.

In `split_bead_description_scope` (service.rs:563-588), fenced code blocks in the bead description are not handled. A bead description containing a code example like:

```
Here is an example:
\`\`\`
Non-goals:
- this is code, not a real section
\`\`\`
```

would incorrectly trigger non-goals extraction from inside the fence. Add fence tracking (similar to `validate_canonical_prompt_shape`) to skip lines inside code blocks when detecting section boundaries.

`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:568-581` exit `NonGoals`/`AcceptanceCriteria` mode on any markdown heading, not just another canonical section. A bead description like `Non-goals:\n### Examples\n- do not change CLI` will reset `active_section` at `### Examples`, so the later bullets fall back into `Must-Do Scope` and become in-scope work in the rendered prompt. The added coverage at `tests/unit/project_run_record_test.rs:804-845` only exercises plain subsection labels, so this markdown-heading case is still untested.

Fence delimiter lines pushed into `non_goal_lines` will be mangled by `collect_non_goal_items` (`service.rs:575-583`). When a fenced code block appears inside a Non-goals section, the ``` delimiter lines and interior code lines are all pushed to `non_goal_lines` and then parsed by `collect_non_goal_items` as prose/bullets, producing garbled non-goal items (e.g., a line like `\`\`\`md` becomes a standalone non-goal item). Either strip fence delimiters before calling `collect_non_goal_items`, or make `collect_non_goal_items` fence-aware, or add a test documenting the current behavior as intentional.

The `is_fence_delimiter` check (`starts_with("\`\`\`")`) does not track the opening fence length, so a 4-backtick opening fence can be incorrectly closed by a 3-backtick line (CommonMark requires the closing fence to have >= the opening fence's backtick count). This affects both `service.rs:487` and `task_prompt_contract.rs:67`. Consider tracking the opening fence length, or at minimum add a code comment documenting this known limitation.

`src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:184-191`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:245-253`, and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:319-320` make canonical-contract drift a hard terminal failure instead of a retryable prompt-review concern. Validators are rendered from `refinement.refined_prompt`, so if the refiner drops or reorders the contract marker/headings they stop receiving the contract-preservation instructions on that round. If those validators still accept, the post-acceptance `validate_canonical_prompt_shape` check immediately returns `PromptReviewRejected`, bypassing the existing `prior_concerns` retry path even when `max_refinement_retries > 0`. A transient formatting miss on a bead-backed prompt can therefore abort prompt review instead of being fed back to the refiner.

`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:612-617`, `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:634-661`, and `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:699-718` render free-form summary/description/list values by prefixing them with `- ` but do not indent or escape continuation lines. `MilestoneBundle.executive_summary` is only required to be non-empty (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:100-101`), so perfectly valid multiline milestone text can inject raw top-level lines or `## ...` headings into the generated prompt. Because `create_project_from_bead_context` now validates the canonical shape (`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:384-393`), that multiline content can corrupt the contract or make `project create-from-bead` fail outright. The renderer needs to preserve multiline fields as blocks or indent continuation lines before validation.

Special-case bullet fields whose first content line is a fence opener. `collect_non_goal_items` now preserves raw fenced blocks (`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:548-570`), but `render_bullet_item` still emits the first line inline as `- ```md` (`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:669-679`). `validate_canonical_prompt_shape` only recognizes a fence when the trimmed line itself starts with backticks or tildes (`src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:177-190`), so it misses that opener, treats the closing fence as a new opener, and then skips the remaining canonical headings. A non-goal or other bullet-rendered field that begins with a fenced example will therefore generate a canonical prompt that `create_project_from_bead_context` rejects (`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:384-389`). Render first-line fences on a new indented line, or teach the validator to recognize list-prefixed fence openers.

Restore trailing-whitespace tolerance for canonical headings. `canonical_section_heading_index` now matches with `line.strip_prefix("## ")` (`src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:66-70`), so headings like `## Review Policy ` or `## Milestone Summary ` are no longer recognized even though they are equivalent Markdown headings. That stricter match feeds both canonical validation and prompt-review drift enforcement (`src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:163-220`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:320-330`), so a refiner can preserve the contract semantically and still get forced into retry/reject loops because of harmless suffix whitespace. Keep the new column-0 requirement if you want to ignore indented body text, but trim the line ending before comparing.

Extract the duplicated `FenceDelimiter` struct, `opening_fence_delimiter`, and `closes_fence` functions (identically defined in both `service.rs:478-518` and `task_prompt_contract.rs:16-98`) into a single shared private module within `project_run_record` (e.g., `fence_util.rs` or a `pub(crate)` section in `task_prompt_contract.rs`). Both call-sites should import from the shared location.

`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:618-627` now strips every line after a `## Acceptance Criteria` marker out of `Must-Do Scope`, but `src/cli/project.rs:332-333` only populates `bead_acceptance_criteria` from the structured field. Many existing beads still carry their acceptance criteria only in the description, for example `.beads/issues.jsonl:1` (`ralph-burning-0gn`). For those beads the generated prompt now says `No explicit acceptance criteria were supplied.` and silently drops the real acceptance criteria. Preserve description-sourced acceptance criteria when the structured field is empty, or stop deleting that section unless it has been materialized into `bead_acceptance_criteria`.

`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:618-627` leaves `active_section` set to `NonGoals` until `Acceptance Criteria`, so any later subsection is reclassified as out-of-scope. Existing beads already use `Non-goals:` followed by required `Contract hardening notes:` blocks, for example `.beads/issues.jsonl:17` and `.beads/issues.jsonl:30`. The new renderer will move those hardening notes into `## Explicit Non-Goals`, telling downstream agents not to do required work. Reset the parser when a new non-non-goal subsection starts, or parse only the explicit non-goal bullets instead of treating the whole tail of the description as non-goals.

In `is_bead_description_subsection_boundary` (service.rs:589-591), `line.trim().ends_with(':')` is overly broad — it matches any line ending in a colon, including things like `Example usage:` or `Note:` inside a Non-Goals or Acceptance Criteria section. While the `previous_line_blank` guard prevents most false positives, a non-goal section containing a blank line followed by a line like `Details:` or `Context:` would be incorrectly treated as a subsection boundary, prematurely ending the section and routing subsequent lines into must-do scope. Consider requiring the line to be a standalone label (e.g., no leading bullet prefix, not indented, maybe at least 2 words or matching a stricter pattern) to reduce false-positive risk.

`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:772-776` and `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:824-827` emit `Must-Do Scope` verbatim, but `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:158-188` treats any unindented canonical heading as a real contract section. A bead description that includes an example heading like `## Review Policy` or `## Milestone Summary` now makes the generated prompt fail its own validation path in `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:385-394`, so `project create-from-bead` rejects otherwise valid bead text.

`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:550-569` only splits extracted section items on `- ` / `* ` bullets. If embedded `Non-goals` or `Acceptance Criteria` use a numbered Markdown list (`1. ...`, `2. ...`), `collect_section_items` merges the whole block into one bullet when `render_bead_task_prompt` re-emits it via `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:644-645` and `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:780-787`. That silently changes multiple criteria/non-goals into a single item.

`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:488-513` does not normalize a trailing `:` after Markdown headings. As a result, common forms like `## Non-goals:` or `## Acceptance Criteria:` are not recognized as section markers, so their contents stay in `Must-Do Scope` instead of populating the canonical sections. With an empty structured acceptance-criteria field, the prompt can even fall through to the `No explicit acceptance criteria were supplied.` fallback at `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:784-787` even though the description provided them.

Require `{{task_prompt_contract}}` in the override templates, or inject the contract block outside the template body. In `src/contexts/workspace_governance/template_catalog.rs:406-444` the new placeholder is still optional for every affected stage/panel template, and `validate_template` / `render` (`src/contexts/workspace_governance/template_catalog.rs:736-742`, `src/contexts/workspace_governance/template_catalog.rs:824-828`) therefore accept override templates that omit it and expand it to empty. Because the workflow callers only surface the contract through that placeholder (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:159-171`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:351-359`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/completion.rs:81-90`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1587-1595`), any workspace/project with a custom template silently loses the shared prompt-contract guidance.

Make contract detection top-level/fence-aware instead of matching any line anywhere. `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:86-87` flips on the contract whenever the exact marker text appears on any line, while the rest of the shape validator is fence-aware (`src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:145-156`). Downstream helpers and prompt creation reuse that broad check (`src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:113-127`, `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:385-395`). A non-canonical prompt that merely quotes the marker inside a code block or AGENTS snippet will therefore be treated as canonical, causing stage prompts to inject contract instructions and prompt review to enforce canonical-section drift on a prompt that never actually declared the contract.

src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:104-129 and 173-180, plus src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:320-326 and src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:385-394. `prompt_uses_contract()` only recognizes the canonical prompt when the contract marker appears before the first canonical sections, but `validate_canonical_prompt_shape()` only checks that the marker exists somewhere at top level. That means a refiner or prompt override can move `<!-- ralph-task-prompt-contract: bead_execution_prompt/1 -->` to the bottom of the prompt, still pass validation, and then lose all downstream contract guidance because later consumers call `prompt_uses_contract()` and return an empty `task_prompt_contract` block. The validator needs to enforce the same marker-placement rule that detection and downstream consumers rely on.

src/contexts/workspace_governance/template_catalog.rs:406-458 and 750-756, plus src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:109-165 and src/contexts/workflow_composition/completion.rs:73-86. The new `task_prompt_contract` placeholder was made mandatory for every stage/prompt-review/completion/final-review override template. Existing custom overrides that predate this change will now fail template validation with `missing required placeholder`, even though callers already provide the new value and those overrides could otherwise keep rendering exactly as before. This is a runtime compatibility regression outside the prompt-contract behavior itself.

In `task_prompt_contract.rs:validate_canonical_prompt_shape` (lines 181-192), when the contract marker IS at the top of the prompt but both 'Milestone Summary' (index 0) and 'Current Bead Details' (index 1) are missing, `prompt_uses_contract` returns false (since it requires a section with index <= 1 after the marker). This triggers the error 'contract marker must appear before the canonical section block', which is misleading — the marker IS before the remaining sections; the first two are just absent. The user also gets the correct missing-section errors, so the prompt is properly rejected, but the marker-placement error is confusing noise. Consider gating the 'must appear before' error on whether ANY canonical section heading exists after the marker position, rather than relying on `prompt_uses_contract`'s stricter heuristic.

Fix the heading-escaping mismatch in the canonical prompt renderer and validator. `render_bead_task_prompt()` only prefixes embedded body lines with two spaces in `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:696-711` and `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:732-744`, but markdown still treats headings with up to three leading spaces as real headings. Meanwhile `validate_canonical_prompt_shape()` only recognizes headings that start at column 1 in `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:62-67` and `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:199-241`. That means inputs such as ` ## Acceptance Criteria` or ` ## Review Policy` inside milestone/bead prose still pass drift validation even though downstream readers will see extra top-level sections. Escape these lines strongly enough (for example 4+ spaces or fenced blocks), and make the validator reject markdown-valid canonical headings outside the intended section boundaries.

Preserve indentation when extracting embedded `Non-goals` and `Acceptance Criteria` lists from bead descriptions. `collect_section_items()` trims each line before list parsing in `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:569-595`, and `strip_markdown_list_marker()` then treats indented sub-bullets/numbered items as new top-level entries in `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:517-540`. A nested list like `- primary criterion` followed by ` - supporting note` is flattened into two separate acceptance criteria/non-goals in the rendered prompt, which changes the actual scope boundary. Keep indentation context, or only start a new item for list markers that were top-level in the source text.

`src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:252-259` stops reporting canonical-heading duplicates once all expected sections have been seen, while `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:920-923` emits `AGENTS / Repo Guidance` verbatim at top level. That means a prompt override/refinement can append a second top-level canonical section like `## Acceptance Criteria` after `## AGENTS / Repo Guidance` and still pass `validate_canonical_prompt_shape()`. `create_project_from_bead_context()` and prompt-review drift checks will then accept a semantically drifted contract as valid. Tighten validation so extra top-level canonical headings after the contract block are rejected unless they are structurally escaped as section body content.

Replace `DefaultHasher` in `FileSystem::prompt_hash` with an explicit stable digest. `src/adapters/fs.rs:248-255` currently hashes prompt text with `std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher`, and that value is then persisted and compared by prompt-drift/resume logic (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/drift.rs:62-90` and related callers). `DefaultHasher` is not a stable cross-release hash contract, so a Rust/std upgrade can make an unchanged prompt look different and spuriously trigger abort/restart paths. Use a fixed algorithm (for example BLAKE3/SHA-256/xxhash with a pinned implementation) and add a golden stability test.

Make `render_bead_task_prompt` preserve fenced `AGENTS / Repo Guidance` content verbatim. The contract guidance says milestone-provided AGENTS guidance must be preserved verbatim (`src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:160-168`), but the renderer rewrites any line that looks like a canonical `## ...` heading without tracking fenced blocks (`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:707-724`, applied at `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:890-896`). If the milestone guidance contains a fenced example with `## Review Policy`, `## Acceptance Criteria`, etc., the prompt renderer will indent that line and change the literal snippet. Make the escaping fence-aware, or exempt fenced guidance blocks entirely, and add a regression test for AGENTS guidance that includes fenced canonical headings.

Stop hardcoding `base_dir/.git/ralph-burning-live` in the new test helpers. The implementation intentionally resolves both a real `.git/` directory and a `.git` file that points at another gitdir (`src/adapters/fs.rs:382-406`), but the new helpers in `tests/unit/workspace_test.rs:13-22`, `tests/unit/adapter_contract_test.rs:31-74`, `tests/unit/workflow_engine_test.rs:52-57`, and `tests/run_attach_tmux.rs:22-27` bypass that logic and only model the simple-directory case. That means a regression in `resolve_git_dir` for worktrees/submodules can ship while these updated tests stay green. Route the tests through the production helper, or a shared wrapper around it, instead of duplicating the path convention.

In `split_bead_description_scope` (service.rs ~line 689-696), when `active_section` is `Some` (inside non-goals or acceptance criteria), a subsection boundary reset only fires when `previous_line_blank` is true. This means a non-goal section like:

```
Non-goals:
- item one
Implementation notes:
- this goes back to must-do
```

would NOT reset to must-do scope because there's no blank line before 'Implementation notes:'. This is actually tested and by design (single-word labels stay inside non-goals per tests), but the `is_standalone_plain_subsection_label` requires `split_whitespace().count() >= 2` while `is_bead_description_subsection_boundary` also triggers on markdown headings. A markdown heading like `### Details` (which has only 1 word after the hashes) immediately following non-goals content (no blank line) would NOT trigger the reset either — this seems intentional but underdocumented. Adding a brief comment explaining the blank-line gate would help future maintainers.

Compatibility regression: `src/adapters/fs.rs:249-253` changes `FileSystem::prompt_hash` to SHA-256, but `src/contexts/workflow_composition/drift.rs:62-90` still compares the freshly computed hash to the persisted `prompt_hash_at_cycle_start` with exact equality. Any interrupted or paused run created before this change will now look like prompt drift even when `prompt.md` is unchanged, causing bogus continue/restart/abort behavior on resume. Preserve the previous persisted hash format or add an explicit migration/dual-hash compatibility path for legacy snapshots/project records.

In `src/adapters/fs.rs:258-265`, `legacy_prompt_hash` uses `std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher` whose algorithm is explicitly not guaranteed stable across Rust compiler versions. If the project upgrades Rust and `DefaultHasher` changes (as Rust docs warn it may), legacy hashes persisted in existing project snapshots will silently fail to match, causing false drift detection on resume. The pinned test value (`5b624debc08968f4`) catches this at test time but does not prevent breakage for in-flight projects. Consider using an explicit `siphasher::SipHasher13` (or equivalent fixed-algorithm hasher) to make the legacy compatibility path truly stable, or document the known fragility and intended migration timeline.

When `prompt_matches_resume_baseline` matches via the legacy hash path and returns `NoChange` (drift.rs:64-68), neither the `project.toml` prompt_hash nor the `ActiveRun.prompt_hash_at_cycle_start` is migrated to the new SHA-256 hash. This means projects that never trigger `Continue` or `RestartCycle` will recompute the legacy hash on every resume indefinitely, never naturally migrating to SHA-256. Consider adding a lightweight migration in the `NoChange` path (e.g., updating `project.toml` and the active run's cycle-start hash to SHA-256 when the legacy fallback fires) to ensure all projects converge to the stable hash algorithm.

`src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:128-145` makes `prompt_uses_contract()` return `true` only after the marker is followed by `## Milestone Summary` or `## Current Bead Details`. If a canonical bead prompt drifts by losing either of those early headings but still keeps the top-level marker and later canonical sections, the workflow stops treating it as contract-bound. That disables the contract guidance injected from `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:109-110`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/completion.rs:79-80`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1585-1586`, and it also bypasses prompt-review drift enforcement in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:320-325`. The fix is to treat any top-level contract marker as opting into contract enforcement, then let `validate_canonical_prompt_shape()` report the missing headings.

`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:586-608` finalizes the current extracted non-goal / acceptance item on every blank line before checking whether the next line is an indented continuation of the same Markdown list item. That means a valid multi-paragraph list item such as `- criterion` followed by a blank line and an indented explanatory paragraph is rewritten into multiple bullets in the generated canonical prompt. Preserve blank-line-plus-indented continuation paragraphs within the current item instead of unconditionally splitting on blank lines.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'final_review:reviewer' via 'claude': claude exited with code 1

details.body: In the Claude `finish` method, the last-resort fallback extraction path (line ~189, the `extract_json_from_text(&stdout_text)` branch that fires when both `structured_output` and `result` are empty) does NOT call `unwrap_claude_structured_output_payload` on the extracted value. This is inconsistent with the `structured_output` path (line 158) and the `result` path (line 164), both of which call the unwrapper. If Claude returns a `{"data": <payload>}` wrapper via raw stdout text instead of the normal channels, the downstream consumer will receive the wrapper object instead of the actual contract payload. Apply the same `unwrap_claude_structured_output_payload(val)` call at line 189.

Add direct unit tests for `is_claude_structured_output_retry_failure`. The function has two detection branches (the `subtype` check and the `errors` array check) plus a negative path (valid JSON that doesn't match either pattern), but no dedicated tests. The retry logic is guarded behind `request.prior_session.is_some()` and a non-zero exit, making it hard to exercise end-to-end. A few small unit tests calling the function directly with representative JSON payloads (positive subtype match, positive errors-array match, negative non-matching JSON, negative non-JSON stdout) would lock in the detection heuristics against regressions.

`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:689-694` still tells the model to "Return ONLY valid JSON" matching the original contract schema, but `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:707-710` now changes Claude's actual `--json-schema` to `{ "data": <contract> }` before that same stdin payload is passed to Claude at `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:741`. `unwrap_claude_structured_output_payload()` at `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:157-164` only runs after the CLI has already accepted the response, so it cannot rescue outputs that follow the prompt text instead of the wrapped schema. This leaves Claude with two incompatible top-level contracts and can itself trigger the `error_max_structured_output_retries` failures the patch is trying to recover from. Derive the stdin schema from the same wrapped/transformed contract, or explicitly describe the wrapper in the prompt, so the prompt contract and transport contract stay identical.

In `unwrap_claude_structured_output_payload` (process_backend.rs:1626-1633), the heuristic of unwrapping any single-key JSON object with a `"data"` key is fragile. If Claude fails to follow the wrapped schema and returns the raw contract payload directly — and that raw payload happens to be `{"data": <value>}` — the unwrap silently strips the legitimate key. Consider a more explicit marker (e.g., a sentinel key like `"__rb_wrapped": true` alongside `"data"`) or check during unwrapping that the inner value validates against the original contract schema, rather than relying solely on the single-key heuristic.

The `assemble_stdin` refactor (process_backend.rs:680) changes the schema shown in the stdin prompt for non-Claude backends (Codex, OpenRouter). Previously `assemble_stdin` called `request.contract.json_schema_value()` (raw schema without strict-mode enforcement or ref inlining). Now it receives the fully processed schema from `contract_schema_for_backend`. For Codex/OpenRouter, this means the prompt text now includes `"additionalProperties": false` annotations and inlined `$ref` definitions that were previously absent. Add a test that verifies non-Claude stdin schemas do NOT include the `properties.data` wrapper, and document the intentional behavior change of showing processed schemas in stdin prompts.

In `is_claude_structured_output_retry_failure` (process_backend.rs:1593-1613), the function does not check `is_error: true` before inspecting `subtype` or `errors` fields. While the calling context is gated by `!status.success()`, a non-error stdout JSON response that coincidentally contains `{"subtype": "error_max_structured_output_retries"}` or an `errors` array with the matching substring would trigger an unintended session-drop retry. Guard the function with an `is_error` check for consistency with `extract_stdout_error`.

Blocking: `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:668-752` now normalizes and, for Claude, wraps the transport schema before it is appended to stdin, but the workflow prompt builders still render the raw schema into the prompt body via `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:107-108` and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:159-168`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:396-405`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/completion.rs:269-272`, and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1583-1594`; that block is still presented to the model as authoritative by `src/contexts/workspace_governance/template_catalog.rs:112-118`. As a result, every process-backed invocation now carries two different “authoritative” schemas. For Claude this is still a real correctness bug, because the adapter schema is now `{ "__rb_wrapped": true, "data": ... }` while the workflow prompt still shows the unwrapped payload shape, so the model is being given incompatible top-level contracts in the same request. Update the prompt generators/panel consumers to use the same backend-processed schema (or remove the duplicate schema block entirely), and add an end-to-end test that exercises a built stage/panel prompt through `ProcessBackendAdapter` for Claude.
